one_liner: NVDA at $192.53 is touching the Bollinger Band lower ($191.23) and sitting exactly at the 90-day support zone — RSI 34.5 approaching oversold with a HOLD signal active; the structure says watch for a bounce, not a breakdown.

price_action.vol_rank: #33 of 90 sessions — unremarkable volume, normal activity
price_action.intraday_story: NVDA opened at $193.12, briefly touched $195.55, then sold off to a low of $191.22 — precisely at the Bollinger Band lower ($191.23) — before recovering to close at $192.53. The stock found buyers at the BB lower and the close is above the intraday low, suggesting short-term support held. Volume was exactly average (1.05x), which means this is low-conviction selling, not institutional distribution.

news_catalyst.headline: No NVDA-Specific Catalyst — Broad Tech Weakness Pressuring AI Names
news_catalyst.summary: No Nvidia-specific news today. The 1.64% decline is part of a broader AI-adjacent stock rotation cited in multiple outlets (SpaceX, Tesla, Alphabet all declining in a 'broad tech rout'). The narrative context: Oracle had its worst week since the dot-com bust and Alphabet is down on AI talent-drain worries, which is creating sector-wide pressure. NVDA is simply moving with the group. The HOLD signal (entry $189-$195, stop $179, target $212) remains active and in-the-money on the entry range — current price is within the signal's entry zone.

technical.summary:
NVDA is touching the Bollinger Band lower at $191.23 — today's intraday low of $191.22 is to-the-penny at that level. This is a natural mean-reversion support zone. RSI at 34.5 is one tick above the traditional 30 oversold threshold. MACD is in a bearish crossover but the histogram has been compressing (losing bearish momentum). Volume at 1.05x is entirely unremarkable — there's no institutional selling pressure visible. This is a cooling, not a breakdown.

The 90-day Wyckoff support level is at $175.50 — still 8.8% below current price, providing substantial buffer. The stock is in neutral consolidation between $175 support and $229 resistance. No structural signals in either direction. The HOLD signal entry zone ($189-$195) brackets current price precisely.

For confirmation: a close back above $195 on any session would suggest the BB lower bounce is holding and the short-term correction is over. A close below $191 on volume above 250M would signal accelerating institutional selling toward the $175 support.

wyckoff_narrative:
NVDA shows no Wyckoff events in the 90-day dataset — it's in a neutral consolidation phase with no confirmed accumulation or distribution structure. The 0/5 readings on both accumulation and distribution signals mean the Wyckoff framework is explicitly saying 'no dominant structural bias' — neither bulls nor bears have established control.

The stock's price history in the dataset: a strong uptrend from below $175 to above $229 (resistance), followed by a pullback and now consolidation. This is potentially a 'Reaccumulation' phase — a mid-markup rest before the next leg up. The key signal to watch: if NVDA can hold the $175-195 range on light volume and then push through $229 resistance on elevated volume (above 300M), that would be a Sign of Strength confirming the reaccumulation thesis. The HOLD signal's $212 target aligns with a test of $210-215 overhead resistance before tackling $229.

valuation.summary: DCF base case of $334 implies 73% upside — compelling. Crucially, the bear case at $192 is essentially the current price, meaning you're buying the bear case for free when you add the base and bull scenarios. The 15% discount rate and 50% growth assumption are conservative for NVDA given AI accelerator demand; at 12% discount rate the intrinsic value would be substantially higher.

key_strengths: Dominant AI accelerator market share — estimated 80%+ in training workloads
50% projected FCF growth rate with 75%+ gross margins
key_concerns: Single-product concentration risk on H100/H200/B200 series
fundamentals.summary: Grade A fundamentals with a structural competitive moat in AI compute. High leverage is the only flag, offset by exceptional cash generation. The bear case DCF at $192 is today's price — the stock is priced for the worst case, not the most likely case. Fundamentals strongly support a hold/accumulate stance on any pullback to BB lower.

risks: Intraday low of $191.22 precisely touched BB lower ($191.23) — if next session opens below $191 and stays there, BB support has failed and $175 becomes the next target
MACD bearish crossover persists — no technical confirmation of trend reversal yet
Broader AI sector pressure (Oracle worst week since dot-com, Alphabet down) could extend before rotating back
